explicit_text: Before I entered the service, I was living in LaGrange, Illinois, where I had just finished high school in June. That summer, I mostly spent time with some of my boyfriends and my high school baseball teammates who later went into the service with me. LaGrange was my home base before I was drafted into the Army in the fall at age 18.

explicit_text: I was inducted into the United States Army at Fort Sheridan, right in Chicago, shortly after graduating high school. The first days there were mostly medical exams and testing in a large room with many other men. It was the place where I officially entered the Army before heading off to basic training.

explicit_text: After induction, I was sent to a camp near Little Rock, Arkansas, for my basic training. We traveled there by troop train, which took about a day or a day and a half. The camp was about twenty miles from the train station, and we were taken there by trucks. I spent seventeen weeks there learning all the basic skills needed for the Army.

explicit_text: I was drafted into the United States Army right after high school in 1944. My induction was at Fort Sheridan, and I went through seventeen weeks of basic training near Little Rock, Arkansas. I learned practical skills like digging foxholes, handling the M1 Garand rifle, and jungle and landing craft training later on. The Army was a big change from civilian life, but I made it through with the support of my high school teammates who were drafted with me.

explicit_text: During basic training, I learned to use the M1 Garand, which was the standard Army rifle. I had never handled a gun before except for a BB gun, so I took classes on shooting, cleaning, and maintaining it. I even won a sharpshooting medal with the M1, which was a proud moment for me during those seventeen weeks of training.

explicit_text: After my two-week leave following basic training, I traveled with my two buddies to San Francisco, California. We stayed at an Army-used resort on the Pacific Ocean for about five days while awaiting further orders. It was a brief stop before we moved on to Portland and eventually shipped out to the Pacific theater.

explicit_text: From San Francisco, I went to Portland, Oregon, where I stayed only a couple of days before shipping out. We boarded an old Javanese ship there, and the ocean conditions were rough, especially during winter. It was my first time on a ship, and I got seasick almost immediately as we headed out into the Pacific Ocean.

The vastness of the Pacific was daunting, but it was the route to where we would be fighting and training for the war.
